prompt:
Ticket: internal notes service, stdlib only. We embed this in an appliance image, so no Flask, no FastAPI, no pip anything -- `http.server` is fine. Entry point is `server.py`:

    python3 server.py --db notes.json --port 0

Bind 127.0.0.1 only (never 0.0.0.0). `--port 0` means the OS picks a free port; whatever port you actually got, the FIRST line on stdout must be exactly `listening on 127.0.0.1:<port>` and it must be flushed immediately -- our supervisor parses that line to wire up the proxy. Requests and responses are JSON (Content-Type: application/json). A note looks like {"id": int, "title": str, "body": str, "version": int}.

Routes:
- GET /notes -- every note, sorted by id. Optional ?q=term filters to notes whose title or body contains the term, case-insensitively.
- POST /notes with {"title", "body"} -- 201, assigns the next id (ids start at 1 and are NEVER reused, even after deletes or restarts), version 1, returns the note.
- GET /notes/<id> -- 200, returns the note with an ETag header of the version in quotes, e.g. ETag: "3".
- PUT /notes/<id> with {"title", "body"} -- optimistic concurrency, this is the whole point of the rewrite: the client must send If-Match with the note's current ETag. No If-Match header at all -> 428. If-Match that doesn't match the current version -> 412 and the note stays untouched. Match -> apply the update, bump version by 1, 200 with the updated note and the new ETag.
- DELETE /notes/<id> -- 204 on success.

Unknown note id (or any unknown path) -> 404. Unparseable JSON body, or a missing/empty title on create -> 400. Every error response body is a JSON object with an "error" key -- no HTML error pages, clients choke on them.

Persistence: write the store to the --db file after every successful mutation. The supervisor SIGTERMs us without warning, and a restarted server pointed at the same file must serve identical data and keep handing out ids where it left off.

Structure the code properly -- HTTP handler wiring, the store/persistence, and validation should not be one giant file. `python3 test_notes_api.py` is the acceptance gate (it boots your server as a subprocess, talks to it over HTTP, kills it, and restarts it). Do not modify the test file.
